title: Lab 01: Networking And Hostname
description: Configure persistent networking and hostname settings on client in RHCSA style

tasks:
Configure the active client connection with the following persistent settings:

IP Address: 192.168.122.25
Netmask: 255.255.255.0
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS Server: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: client.netlab.local

Add a persistent hosts entry with the following value:

Hostname: repo.netlab.local
Address: 192.168.122.3
Verify that the active connection comes back with the same values after reconnecting it.
hints: Use nmcli or nmtui for the network profile.
Use hostnamectl for the persistent hostname.
checks: test "$(nmcli -g connection.interface-name connection show "System eth1")" = 'eth1' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "System eth1")" = '192.168.122.25/24' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "System eth1")" = '192.168.122.1' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "System eth1")" = '192.168.122.3' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "System eth1")" = 'manual' && test "$(nmcli -g connection.autoconnect connection show "System eth1")" = 'yes'
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.25/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
